LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JASON ROGERS' MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. CCP 128.7 MONETARY SANCTIONS REGARDING PRAYERS FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IN THE ANSWERS OF DEFENDANTS BRIAN AND JEAN LEE IS DENIED AND THE LEES' REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS ALSO DENIED. ALTHOUGH THERE IS ARGUABLE LEGAL AND FACT BASES FOR BOTH MR. ROGERS' MOTION AND THE LEES' PRAYERS FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND THUS NEITHER SIDE IS ENTITLED TO SANCTIONS PER CCP 128.7, THERE IS LITTLE DOUBT THAT BOTH THIS MOTION AND THE LEES' PRAYERS FOR FEES WERE UNNECESSARY AND ONLY ADDED TO THE COST OF THIS ALREADY SEVERELY OVER-LITIGATED AND EXTRAORDINARILY EXPENSIVE CASE AS WELL AS THE PARTIES AND POSSIBLY THEIR ATTORNEYS' STRONG ADVERSE FEELINGS FOR EACH OTHER. THE MOTION WAS UNNECESSARY BECAUSE THE LEES' PRAYERS FOR FEES ARE NOT SELF-EXECUTING. MR. ROGERS WILL HAVE AMPLE NOTICE AND OPPORTUNITY TO OPPOSE A FEES REQUEST BY THE LEES, EVEN ASSUMING THEY EVER MAKE ONE AGAIN. THE PRAYERS FOR FEES WERE UNNECESSARY BECAUSE, EVEN IN THE ABSENCE OF THE PRAYERS, THE LEES ARE ABLE TO SEEK FEES PER THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE AND THE COST OF PROOF PROVISIONS OF THE DISCOVERY STATUTES. THE PARTIES AND THEIR COUNSEL ARE ADMONISHED TO LOOK FOR WAYS TO DECREASE THE HEAT AND COST OF THIS LITIGATION, RATHER THAN THE OPPOSITE. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of The Courts January 10, 2018 Denial Of Plaintiffs Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-28-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ronald M. Toran, Esq., Tel. 415.919.9033, for plaintiff/moving party;
Melissa M. Malstrom, Esq. of Bledsoe Diestel Treppa & Crane, Tel. 415.981.5411 for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF JASON ROGERS' Motion For Reconsideration Of The Courts January 10, 2018 Denial Of Plaintiff's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint is DENIED. The sole asserted changed circumstance proffered in support of this motion is that Jean Lee is no longer a party to the case. While that is true, the January 10 order was based on far more than Ms. Lee being prejudiced by allowing Mr. Rogers leave to file his proposed third amended complaint. Among other things, the January 10 order states that Mr. Rogers' proposed new claims are not "actionable" and that granting leave would require a delay of the trial (now only more true than in January) to the prejudice of both of the Lees. Ms. Lee being dismissed from this case does not overcome these additional reasons why the original motion was denied or warrant reconsideration of the ruling on that motion.

Defendant's oral request for sanctions is denied.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court received an email withdrawing the contest email. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRIAN LEE, JEAN LEE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Defendant Brian Lee's motions for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety and defendant Jean Lee's motion for summary judgment is granted. There is a triable dispute whether Mr. Lee's conduct was privileged and bars the four causes of action for slander, libel, intentional infliction of emotional distress, and negligence alleged against him. Assuming that Mr. Lee and the recipients of the publications had a common interest regarding safety at the premises, there are triable issues whether the publications were motivated by Mr. Lee's ill will towards Mr. Rogers and whether Mr. Lee lacked reasonable grounds for belief in the truth of the publications. (Noel v. River Hills Wilsons, Inc. (2003) 113 Cal.App.4th 1363.) The triable issues regarding malice are based on a) Mr. Lee telling Mr. Rogers that he did not want him living on the premises (Rogers Depo., 49:21-50:7); b) Mr. Lee sending e-mails to Arun Goel stating that there will continue to be problems unless the Goels sell the property (Arun Goel Dec., Exs. 5 and 6); and c) "Jason M. Rogers" is a common name and Intelius' disclaimers that the information may not be accurate, and Mr. Lee not making any investigation to determine whether plaintiff Jason Rogers was in fact indicted for threatening a federal judge (Toran Dec. Ex. 4; Rogers Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. 5); While Mr. Lee correctly notes that "[w]here the publication comes from a known reliable source and there is nothing in the circumstances to suggest inaccuracy, there is no duty to investigate" (Reader's Digest Assn. v. Sup. Crt. (1984) 37 Cal.3d 254, 259), the Intelius disclaimers expressly warned of possible inaccuracies. A reasonable trier of fact could find that Mr. Lee was reckless in stating that Mr. Rogers failed to disclose his prior crimes to the Bureau of Real Estate as the stipulation and waiver which Mr. Lee relied on makes no such finding. As to the intentional infliction claim, there is also a triable issue whether Mr. Lee's conduct caused Mr. Rogers to suffer severe emotional distress. Mr. Lee mischaracterizes Mr. Rogers' deposition testimony at 94:22-95:25 and competent evidence supports Mr. Rogers' claimed damages. Ms. Lee is entitled to summary judgment in her favor. The undisputed evidence establishes that she knew nothing about these e-mails until she was carbon copied on them. (Brian Lee Dec., par. 23; Jean Lee Dec., par. 6.) Mr. Rogers fails to present any evidence to create a triable issue regarding the formation of a conspiracy to defame and/or otherwise harm him.

Brian Lee and Jean Lee's orders are signed. (see scanned orders for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Plaintiff Jason Myung Rogers Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ronald M. Toran, Esq., Tel. 415.919.9033, for plaintiff/moving party;
Melissa M. Malstrom, Esq. of Bledsoe Diestel Treppa & Crane, Tel. 415.981.5411 for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JASON MYUNG ROGERS' Motion For Leave To File A Third Amended Complaint is DENIED. While there is a policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings, that policy does not control where, as here, the proposed new claims are not cognizable and/or granting the motion would likely require a continuance of the trial to the prejudice of the opposing party. Mr. Rogers' proposed claims of negligence and intentional infliction of emotional distress against Intelius are not actionable and are clearly designed not to seek relief against Intelius, but rather to prompt Intelius to file a cross-complaint against defendants Brian and Jean Lee. Under the circumstances of this case, where Intelius significantly qualified how its report can be used, as a matter of law Intelius either did not owe any duty sounding in negligence to Mr. Rogers or did not breach that duty and, in all events, Intelius did not act with extreme and outrageous conduct, which is a required element for an IIED claim. Mr. Rogers' papers make clear that he is hoping and expecting that his third amended complaint will result in a cross-complaint against the Lees. That is not a legitimate ground to seek to amend, especially since both sides acknowledge that granting this motion will likely delay the trial, leading to likely further litigation in this already over-litigated case. Any delay of the trial, particularly given Ms. Lee's health, is prejudicial to the Lees.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS
Off Calendar Nov-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Ronald M Toran of the law offices of Ronald M Toran (415-919-9033) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Melissa M. Malstrom] of the law offices of Bledsoe Diestel Treppa & Crane LLP (415-981-5411) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF JASON ROGERS' MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS, CCP 128.7 monetary sanctions regarding prayers for attorney's fees in the answers of defendants Brian and Jean Lee is DENIED and the Lees' request for monetary sanctions is also denied. Although there is arguable legal and fact bases for both Mr. Rogers' motion and the Lees' prayers for attorney's fees and thus neither side is entitled to sanctions per CCP 128.7, there is little doubt that both this motion and the Lees' prayers for fees were unnecessary and only added to the cost of this already severely over-litigated and extraordinarily expensive case as well as the parties and possibly their attorneys' strong adverse feelings for each other. The motion was unnecessary because the Lees' prayers for fees are not self-executing. Mr. Rogers will have ample notice and opportunity to oppose a fees request by the Lees, even assuming they ever make one again. The prayers for fees were unnecessary because, even in the absence of the prayers, the Lees are able to seek fees per the anti-SLAPP statute and the cost of proof provisions of the discovery statutes. The parties and their counsel are admonished to look for ways to decrease the heat and cost of this litigation, rather than the opposite. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Notice Of And Motion For Recovery Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Feb-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ronald M. Toran, Attorney at Law, Tel. 415.919.9033, for plaintiffs;
Jeff V. Ta, of Bledsoe, Diestel, Treppa & Crane LLP, Tel. 415.981.5411, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BRIAN LEE Motion For Recovery Of Attorneys Fees And Costs is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. In granting defendant's CCP 425.16 motion in part while denying it in other parts, the Court ruled that defendant was entitled to attorney's fees and costs "in part." Where results are thus mixed, it is the defendant's obligation to provide sufficient information to permit the Court to allocate the fees and costs. (ComputerExpress, Inc. v. Jackson (2001) 93 Cal.App.4th 993, 1020.) Defendant here has failed to do so, instead opting to seek all fees and costs incurred. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard B.
